User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the Kairos OS system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

role.description: User's role within the system (e.g., Admin Master, Creative).

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client in the Kairos OS system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

TaskType.title: TaskType
TaskType.description: Represents a type of task (e.g., Reel, Post, Video Cinematográfico).

id.description: Unique identifier for the TaskType entity.

color.description: Color associated with the task type (Hex code).

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a task in the Kairos OS system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task entity.

userId.description: Reference to User. (Relationship: User 1:N Task).  Assigned User

name.description: Name or description of the task.

status.description: Current status of the task (e.g., Pending, In Progress, Completed).

sprintId.description: Reference to Sprint. If null, the task is in the backlog.

Quotation.title: Quotation
Quotation.description: Represents a quotation in the Kairos OS system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quotation entity.

validityDays.description: Number of days the quotation is valid for.

createdAt.description: Date and time when the quotation was created.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents an invoice in the Kairos OS system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

invoiceDate.description: Date when the invoice was issued.

description.description: Description of services or products invoiced.

Pack.title: Pack
Pack.description: Represents a predefined pack of services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Pack entity.

name.description: Name of the pack (e.g., Pack Social Media Starter).

taskTypeQuantities.description: Quantities for each TaskType included in the pack.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Represents an expense in the Kairos OS system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

description.description: Description of the expense (e.g., Adobe license, Internet bill).

CreditPurchase.title: CreditPurchase
CreditPurchase.description: Represents a credit purchase with payment schedule.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CreditPurchase entity.

description.description: Description of the purchased item (e.g., Camera Sony Z10).

numberOfMonths.description: Number of months for the payment schedule.

startDate.description: Start date of the payment schedule.

Sprint.title: Sprint
Sprint.description: Represents a sprint in the Kairos OS system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Sprint entity.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Authorization is based on path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores tasks associated with a specific user. Authorization is based on path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier of the task.

definition.description: Stores client information.  Managed through a membership model on each document.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the client.

definition.description: Stores quotations associated with a specific client. Includes denormalized 'clientId' for authorization independence.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the client.

params.name: quotationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the quotation.

definition.description: Stores invoices associated with a specific client. Includes denormalized 'clientId' for authorization independence.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the client.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the invoice.

params.name: taskTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the task type.

params.name: packId
params.description: The unique identifier of the pack.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the expense.

params.name: creditPurchaseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the credit purchase.

params.name: sprintId
params.description: The unique identifier of the sprint.

definition.description: Marks a user as an admin. Existence of document confers admin role.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the Kairos OS application, focusing on authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. The structure leverages path-based ownership for user-specific data and membership maps for collaborative data access. Denormalization is used extensively to ensure authorization independence, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**

*   **User Data:** The `users/{userId}` collection stores user profiles. This is private data, accessible only to the user with the matching `userId`. No denormalization is needed here as authorization is based solely on the path.
*   **Tasks:** Tasks are stored in a subcollection `/users/{userId}/tasks/{taskId}`. This structure establishes clear ownership and avoids the need to check parent documents for authorization.
*   **Clients:** Clients are stored in the `clients/{clientId}` collection. Authorization is managed through a membership model on each document.
*   **Quotations, Invoices:** These are client-owned and stored in `/clients/{clientId}/quotations/{quotationId}` and `/clients/{clientId}/invoices/{invoiceId}`, respectively. The `clientId` is denormalized into each document, making list operations and data access rules simple and independent.
*   **TaskTypes, Packs, Expenses, CreditPurchases, Sprints:** These are stored in root-level collections. Access control is determined by user roles, managed through the `roles_admin/{uid}` collection or team memberships denormalized into these documents where necessary.

**QAPs (Rules Are Not Filters):**

The structure supports secure list operations by segregating data based on access needs. Private user data is stored under `/users/{userId}`, enabling rules that allow users to only list their own data. Membership models on collaborative documents (e.g., Clients) ensure that only authorized users can list and access these documents.

The use of root-level collections for TaskTypes and Packs also enables efficient and secure listing, as security rules can be applied at the collection level without needing to filter based on document content.

**Invariants:**

*   The structure supports ownership invariants through path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/tasks/{taskId}`).
*   Timestamps (e.g., `createdAt` in Quotations) are enforced via rules.
*   Denormalized data (e.g., `clientId` in invoices) ensures data consistency across related documents.

By adhering to these principles and strategies, the data structure provides a secure, scalable, and easily debuggable foundation for the Kairos OS application.
